title: Adyen API credentials should not be disclosed
description: Adyen is a global payment technology company used by major enterprises for payment processing, acquiring, and issuing. Adyen credentials include API keys, client keys, and HMAC signing keys for webhook validation. Exposing these enables unauthorized payment processing and represents a critical PCI-DSS compliance violation.
recommendation: Remove Adyen credentials from source code. Store API keys, client keys, and HMAC keys in environment variables or a secrets manager. Rotate exposed credentials immediately in the Adyen Customer Area. Report PCI-DSS incidents as required.
impact: Unauthorized payment processing, refunds, payouts, and access to transaction data. Major PCI-DSS compliance violation affecting enterprise payment operations.

checks.message: Adyen Live API key detected (AQE prefix). Adyen API keys authenticate payment API calls for charging customers, issuing refunds, managing payouts, and accessing transaction reports. Exposing a live API key allows unauthorized charges to customers, fraudulent refunds, fund transfers, and access to sensitive payment data across your entire merchant account. This is a critical PCI-DSS violation. Revoke immediately in Adyen Customer Area > Developers > API credentials.

Adyen API key detected in variable assignment. Adyen is a major payment processor used by enterprises worldwide. Exposing the API key grants complete control over payment operations including processing charges, refunds, payouts, and accessing transaction data for all payment methods supported by your merchant account. Rotate immediately and assess PCI-DSS incident reporting requirements.

Adyen HMAC webhook signing key detected. Adyen HMAC keys are used to verify the authenticity of webhook notifications from Adyen. Exposing this key allows attackers to forge Adyen webhook events, creating fake payment confirmations, refund notifications, or dispute updates that your application will process as legitimate — potentially allowing fraudulent order fulfillment or financial reconciliation errors. Rotate in Adyen Customer Area > Developers > Webhooks.
